MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-11-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Prior to Open Court, Mr. Conor Nideffer, counsel for Plaintiff appears via Zoom and informs the clerk he has received a contest email from Defense counsel. At the instruction of the Court, the clerk instructs Counsel to send a copy of the contest email via email to the Court.

10:48am During the court recess, Mr. Nideffer states he did not receive a contest email and he was not contesting the tentative ruling.

No Appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CAESARSTONE USA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendant Caesarstone USA, Inc.'s motion to strike is DENIED.

At issue is whether Plaintiffs Fernando Salmeron Reyes and Mirabel Salmeron Aguilar's allegations could support a finding of malice, oppression, or fraud grounding a punitive damages award. (Civ. Code, 3294(a).) "[M]alice does not require actual intent to harm. [Citation.] Conscious disregard for the safety of another may be sufficient where the defendant is aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his or her conduct and he or she willfully fails to avoid such consequences." (Pfeifer v. John Crane, Inc. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299; see also College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 713 ["For plaintiffs attempting to prove malice by showing a 'conscious disregard' of their rights as opposed to an actual intent to harm, the Act imposed additional requirements of 'despicable' and 'willful' defense conduct."].) The court must liberally construe the complaint. (See Code Civ. Proc., 452.)

Here, Plaintiffs allege Defendant intentionally concealed known product dangers, and further allege officer, director and managing agent knowledge/ratification. (See, e.g., Complaint, pgs. 36, 54 and 103-156.) Plaintiffs' allegations are not conclusory. They are sufficient at the pleading stage to support their request for punitive damages.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/jmq)
